sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I understand the mechanic better! Clicking the blue square caused the wall boundary to shift:
- Rows 1-27: wall boundary moved from col 31 to col 27 (wall receded by 4 columns)
- Rows 32-63: wall boundary moved from col 31 to col 55 (wall expanded by 24 columns)
So the blue square acts as a "wall mover" - clicking it shifts the vertical wall boundary. The blue square is at rows 24-27, which is in the upper section. When clicked, it seems to have moved the wall boundary upward on the left side and downward on the right side.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The board has:
1. A vertical wall that can be moved
2. A horizontal black barrier with a yellow gap
3. Blue squares that control wall movement
4. A charcoal+yellow cluster
Maybe the goal is to align the wall boundary with the yellow gap in the black barrier? The yellow gap is at cols 38-39 on rows 28-31. Currently:
- Above the barrier (rows 1-27): wall ends at col 27
- Below the barrier (rows 32-63): wall ends at col 55
The black barrier itself spans cols 20-63 on rows 28-31, with the yellow gap at cols 38-39.
Maybe I need to click the other blue square (rows 32-35) to move the wall in the opposite direction? Let me try that.
Actually, let me think more carefully. The blue square I clicked was at rows 24-27, cols 60-63 (top blue square). After clicking:
- Upper wall boundary: 31 → 27 (moved left by 4)
- Lower wall boundary: 31 → 55 (moved right by 24)
So clicking the top blue square moved the upper wall boundary left and the lower wall boundary right. This seems to be creating a "step" or "staircase" pattern in the wall.
Maybe the goal is to make the wall boundary align with the yellow gap (cols 38-39)? Currently the upper boundary is at 27 and lower is at 55. If I click the bottom blue square, maybe it will move them in the opposite direction.
Let me try clicking the bottom blue square at rows 32-35, cols 60-63.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking the top blue square (rows 24-27) shifted the wall boundary: upper section moved left (31→27), lower section moved right (31→55). Blue squares control wall movement. The yellow gap in the black barrier is at cols 38-39.
Goal model: Likely need to align the wall boundary with the yellow gap or achieve some specific wall configuration.
Plan: Click the bottom blue square (rows 32-35, cols 60-63) to see if it moves the wall in the opposite direction.
